Guideline Description: The agency must Develop, document, and disseminate to designated agency officials (1) An access control policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance (2) Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the access control policy and associated access controls
The agency must Review and update the current (1) Access control policy every three years (or if there is a significant change) (2) Access control procedures at least annually
The agency must Identify and select the accounts with access to FTI to support agency missions/business functions
The agency must Assign account managers for information system accounts
The agency must Establish conditions for group and role membership
The agency must Specify authorized users of the information system, group and role membership, and access authorizations (i.e., privileges) and other attributes (as required) for each account
The agency must Require approval for requests to create information system accounts
The agency must Create, enable, modify, disable, and remove information system accounts in accordance with documented agency account management procedures
The agency must Monitor the use of information system accounts
The agency must Notify account managers when accounts are no longer required, when users are terminated or transferred, or when individual information system usage or need- to-know permission changes

The agency must Authorize access to information systems that receive, process, store, or transmit FTI based on a valid access authorization, need-to-know permission, and under the authority to re-disclosed FTI under the provisions of IRC 6103
The agency must Review accounts for compliance with account management requirements at a minimum of annually for user accounts and semi-annually for privileged accounts
The agency must Establish a process for reissuing shared/group account credentials (if deployed) when individuals are removed from the group.
The information system must enforce Approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies

The information system must enforce A role-based access control policy over defined subjects and objects and controls access to FTI based upon a valid access authorization, intended system usage, and the authority to be disclosed FTI under the provisions of IRC 6103

The information system must enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of FTI within the system and between interconnected systems based on the technical safeguards in place to protect the FTI.
The agency must Separate duties of individuals to prevent harmful activity without collusion
The agency must Document separation of duties of individuals
The agency must Define information system access authorizations to support separation of duties

The agency must Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users (or processes acting on behalf of users) that are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks in accordance with agency missions and business functions
The agency must Explicitly authorize access to FTI (CE1)
The agency must Require that users of information system accounts, or roles, with access to FTI, use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing non-security functions (CE2)
The agency must Restrict privileged accounts on the information system to a limited number of individuals with a need to perform administrative duties (CE5)
The information system must Audit the execution of privileged functions (CE9)
The information system must Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions; including disabling, circumventing, or altering implemented security safeguards/countermeasures (CE10)
The information system must Enforce a limit of three consecutive invalid logon attempts by a user during a 120-minute period
The information system must Automatically lock the account for a period of at least 15 minutes

The information system must Before granting access to the system, display to users an IRS-approved warning banner that provides privacy and security notices consistent with applicable federal laws... Before granting access to the system, display to users an IRS-approved warning banner that provides privacy and security notices consistent with applicable federal laws, Executive Orders, directives, policies, regulations, standards, and guidance and states that: 1. The system contains U.S. Government information 2. Users actions are monitored and audited 3. Unauthorized use of the system is prohibited 4. Unauthorized use of the system is subject to criminal and civil sanctions
The information system must Retain the warning banner on the screen until users acknowledge the usage conditions and take explicit actions to log on to or further access the information system
For publicly accessible systems, the information system must Display the IRS-approved warning banner granting further access

For publicly accessible systems, the information system must Display references, if any, to monitoring, recording, or auditing that are consistent with privacy accommodations for such systems that generally prohibit those activities
For publicly accessible systems, the information system must Include a description of the authorized uses of the system
The information system must Prevent further access to the system by initiating a session lock after 15 minutes of inactivity or upon receiving a request from a user
The information system must Retain the session lock until the user reestablishes access using established identification and authentication procedures
The information system must automatically terminate a user session after 30 minutes of inactivity

Control Test Name: Permitted Actions without Identification or Authentication (AC-14)
Guideline Description: The agency must Identify specific user actions that can be performed on the information system without identification or authentication consistent with agency missions/business functions. FTI may not be disclosed to individuals on the information system without identification and authentication

Control Test Name: Permitted Actions without Identification or Authentication (AC-14)
Guideline Description: The agency must Document and provide supporting rationale in the SSR for the information system the user actions not requiring identification or authentication
The agency must Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of remote access allowed
The agency must Authorize remote access to the information system prior to allowing such connections
The agency must Authorize and document the execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access for compelling operational needs only (CE4)
The information system must Monitor and control remote access methods (CE1)
The information system must Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality and integrity of remote access sessions where FTI is transmitted over the remote connection and (CE2)
The information system must Route all remote accesses through a limited number of managed network access control points (CE3)
The agency must Establish usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for wireless access
The agency must Authorize wireless access to the information system prior to allowing such connections
The agency must Employ a wireless intrusion detection system to identify rogue wireless devices and to detect attack attempts and potential compromises/breaches to the information system (SI-4, CE14)
The information system must protect wireless access to the system using authentication and encryption. (CE1)
The agency must Establish usage restrictions, configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for agency-controlled mobile devices
The agency must Authorize the connection of mobile devices to agency information systems
The agency must Employ encryption to protect the confidentiality and integrity of information on mobile devices (e.g., smartphones and laptop computers) (CE5)

The agency must Purge/wipe information from mobile devices based on 10 consecutive, unsuccessful device logon attempts (e.g., personal digital assistants, smartphones and tablets). Laptop computers are excluded from this requirement (AC-7, CE2)
Unless approved by the Office of Safeguards, the agency must prohibit Access to FTI from external information systems, other than through a virtual desktop infrastructure

Unless approved by the Office of Safeguards, the agency must prohibit Use of agency-controlled portable storage devices (e.g., flash drives, external hard drives) containing FTI on external information systems (CE2)

Unless approved by the Office of Safeguards, the agency must prohibit Use of non-agency-owned information systems; system components; or devices to process, store, or transmit FTI; any non-agency-owned information system usage requires the agency to notify the Office of Safeguards 45 days prior to implementation (CE3)

The agency may allow the use of personally-owned devices, without notification, only for the following purposes Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) used to access e-mail, where all requirements in Section 9.4.8 Mobile Devices are met

The agency may allow the use of personally-owned devices, without notification, only for the following purposes Remote access through a virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI) environment, where all requirements in Section 9.4.13 Virtual Desktop Infrastructure are met
The agency must restrict the sharing/re-disclosure of FTI to only those authorized in IRC 6103 and as approved by the Office of Safeguards.
The agency must Designate individuals authorized to post information onto a publicly accessible information system
The agency must Train authorized individuals to ensure that publicly accessible information does not contain FTI
The agency must Review the proposed content of information prior to posting onto the publicly accessible information system to ensure that FTI is not included
The agency must Review the content on the publicly accessible information system for FTI, at a minimum, quarterly and remove such information, if discovered
